summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #712 where optional enum MCP tool parameters caused LLMs to send literal string 'null' instead of null, by writing a regression test and patching the strict mode schema handling in the moltis providers crate.
summary.outcome: Root cause identified (two-layer bug: canonicalization strips 'type' from enum schemas, then make_nullable had no fallback for enum-only schemas), fix implemented with add_null_to_enum() helper, 4 regression tests added, e2e scenario added, PR #724 created and all lint/clippy issues resolved across two follow-up pushes.

repo: Schema canonicalization in sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat strips 'type': 'string' from enum properties, leaving bare {'enum': [...]} objects — this is valid JSON Schema but breaks assumptions in strict_mode.rs
The collapsible_if and expect_used clippy lints are enforced as errors (-D warnings) across the workspace
Tests in openai_compat use 'let Some(...) = ... else { panic!(...) }' pattern instead of .expect() to satisfy clippy::expect_used lint
The project uses 'beads' (bd) issue tracker alongside GitHub issues; issues need to be claimed and closed via bd commands
just lint uses --features local-llm-metal on macOS; CUDA build failures are expected on macOS and unrelated to code changes
Dolt remote (bd dolt pull) is not configured in worktrees — expected error on commit

code.finding: add_null_to_enum() helper needed collapsible_if fix: nested 'if let' + 'if' must be collapsed to 'if let ... && ...' form
make_nullable() had no handler for enum-only schemas (after canonicalization removes 'type'). Fix adds a final fallback branch that calls add_null_to_enum() when no type/anyOf/oneOf is present
Pre-existing collapsible_if lint: nested if let Some(text) + if !text.is_empty() needed collapsing — fixed as a side effect of running full lint suite
Pre-existing expect_used clippy errors not introduced by this PR — left unresolved as out of scope

workflow: Always run 'just lint' (not just 'just test') before committing — clippy errors only surface during lint
Verify pre-existing failures on main branch before attributing clippy errors to your changes

When canonicalization transforms schemas before strict mode processing, unit tests on the raw patch function can pass while end-to-end tests through the full pipeline fail — always add e2e tests through the full call chain

friction: Clippy collapsible_if and expect_used errors required two additional fix commits after the PR was already created and pushed
The two-layer bug (canonicalization + make_nullable) required a debug test detour to understand what the schema actually looked like after the pipeline
Rate limit hit mid-session ('You've hit your limit · resets 4pm') causing an interruption
Dolt pull error on commit is a persistent noise in the worktree workflow
open_items: Pre-existing expect_used clippy errors in crates/providers/src/async_openai_provider.rs:272 and :299 left unresolved
openai_compat_type_arrays test file has pre-existing expect_used violations not addressed in this PR
